mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding employment income, which boosts aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, causing the S&P 500 to generate positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the average 20-day S&P 500 forward return following a month-over-month increase in PAYEMS is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive monetary tightening regime that increases real interest rates and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to exhibit a negative coefficient with |t-stat| > 2.0 in predicting 20-day forward returns, or the rank information coefficient is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals successful reactivation of idle manufacturing capacity and improving operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-crisis recovery periods where INDPRO accelerates after a 6-month trough, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm broad-based consumer demand strength that lifts revenue expectations across cyclical sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) forces downward revisions to real corporate earnings forecasts and compresses equity valuations through higher expected nominal discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3%, month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL produces a rank information coefficient above -0.03 or a positive mean 20-day forward return over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals supply contraction and falling corporate utilization, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines more than 1% over 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes over any 3-year window, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
